title: Lesson Quiz: Checks and Balances in Action — How the branches limit and influence one another.
description: Assessment of the concept that How the branches limit and influence one another.

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of checks and balances?

choices.text: To allow one branch to control all decisions
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Checks and balances prevents one branch from having total control.

choices.text: To ensure each branch can limit the power of the others
choices.feedback: Correct. Checks and balances allows each branch to limit the others.

choices.text: To eliminate the need for laws
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Laws are still necessary in a constitutional system.

choices.text: To give the judicial branch all authority
choices.feedback: Incorrect. No branch has all authority.

questions.prompt: Checks and balances helps prevent one branch of government from becoming too powerful.

choices.feedback: Correct. This is the purpose of checks and balances.
Incorrect. Checks and balances is designed to prevent too much power in one branch.

questions.prompt: Which situation best shows checks and balances in action?

choices.text: Congress makes a law and no other branch is involved
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Checks and balances involves interaction between branches.

choices.text: The president enforces a law without any limits
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Checks and balances means there are limits.

choices.text: A court reviews a law passed by Congress and determines if it is constitutional
choices.feedback: Correct. This shows the judicial branch limiting the legislative branch.
Incorrect. Voting is civic participation, not checks and balances.

questions.prompt: Checks and balances and separation of powers mean exactly the same thing.

choices.feedback: Incorrect. They are related but not the same.
Correct. Separation of powers divides roles, while checks and balances allows branches to limit each other.

questions.prompt: Why is checks and balances important in a constitutional government?

choices.text: It allows one branch to act without limits
choices.feedback: Incorrect. The system is designed to limit power.

choices.text: It prevents laws from being created
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Laws are still created through the legislative process.

choices.text: It ensures power is shared and prevents abuse of power
choices.feedback: Correct. Checks and balances protects against misuse of power.

choices.text: It removes the need for courts
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Courts are an essential part of the system.
